rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorney Fees
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2021 line 1. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL, THE PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, BY AND Plaintiff City And County Of San Franciscos Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorney Fees is GRANTED in the amount of $197,986. Late filed and oversized oppositions are stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Instructions Re Distribution Of Funds Held By Receiver And Deadline For Filing Of Any Further Motions And Lawsuits By Defendant
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2021 line 2. OTHER KEVIN SINGER Notice Of Motion And Motion For Instructions Re Distribution Of Funds Held By Receiver And Deadline For Filing Of Any Further Motions And Lawsuits By Defendant is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to Set Deadlines for filing any further motions and lawsuits by defendant is GRANTED as follows: any further motions and lawsuits to be filed in accordance with the law, including but not limited to Code of Civil Procedure for post-judgment motions; statute of limitations, etc. Request to stay the disbursement of funds under the judgment "until after the conclusion of the appeal." (Moving papers 4:2-3) is DENIED. Moving party effectively seeks a stay pending appeal without complying with requirements for stay on appeal. Late-filed opposition and untimely joinder previously stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: REXEL USA, INC. VS. LENDLEASE (US) CONSTRUCTION INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Re-Open Discovery For The Limited Purpose Of Preserving Testimony By Deposition
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 22, 2021 line 3. CROSS COMPLAINANT LENDLEASE (US) CONSTRUCTION INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Re-Open Discovery For The Limited Purpose Of Preserving Testimony By Deposition is DENIED without prejudice. Deposition as requested cannot be completed before trial on September 29, 2021. If the trial is continued on the trial call calendar the moving party may re-set the motion for hearing. If the case is assigned to a trial department, the motion may be brought before the trial judge. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
